inline_comments.message: OK. Lets assume that at line 65 exception  is being thrown. It is being caught in catch block, and what then? Does it mean that file has been collected?
Spring has support for SFTP. As this project is based on SpringBoot, which has dependency to Spring, why 3rd party library has been used?

Why not used catching Multiple Exception Types?

Here is example:
https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/technotes/guides/language/catch-multiple.html
Why getter & setter for location as location is being passed in constructor? Can this value be overriden?
Copyright info missing as in other java files
Why in comparison to other files you removed Nokia copyright?
Remove commented out code or use logger
Try to refactor to avoid such long methods

Reomve commented out code.
Please do not commint code which youre are not using currently and which you might try to use latter.
In fact this class does not test anything as all test are commented.
Why did you removed Nokia copyright info?

Against writing class manually I think that better solution is with usage Immutable library. This has been done in prh/datafile collector for dmaap. Take a look in DmaapCustomConfig/DmaapConsumerConfiguration/DmaapPublisherConfiguration class.
This is from first commit :)  Henrik didn't change license information when he was pushing his first code.
Are the trailing spaces here inline with ONAP guidance?
Same question on the trailing spaces here
Think these markers are for tab chars, should probably be spaces.
Should a file marked ".old" be in a repo?
Commenting blank lines is done in only this file. Contrast with (for eg) /datafile-app-server/src/test/java/org/onap/dcaegen2/collectors/datafile/tasks/DmaapPublisherTaskImplTest.java
Commented out code should be removed.
Why "-SNAPSHOT"? Is there no released version? Will this cause issues later?
trailing spaces, mixed tabs and spaces.
trailing spaces, mixed tabs and spaces
trailing spaces, mixed tabs and spaces.
Copyright missing if import statement is correctly added per Maciej's comment
Previous names were "Dmaap", but response files and classes all use "DMaaP"?
Why is there a log file?
"jsonString" and "incorrectJsonString should be in upper snake case to match the style used for "DATAFILE_ENDPOINTS"?
Builder line is very long, can you break it once or more?
Which date format convention is used? US, UK, ISO-8601? Maybe there is guidance on this within ONAP already
These string literals are very long, maven checkstyle complains about this as it exceeds 120 column width. They could be broken using the '+' concatenation operator?
As a utility class with no state, should this methods be static? Then there is no need for the getFtpsClient() method in the DmaapConsumerTaskImpl class
As a utility class with no state, should this method be static? Then there is no need for the getSftpClient() method in the DmaapConsumerTaskImpl class

Static might be appropriate if that method was pure, and even then it would be questionable.
If it is static, then you won't able to unit test the place where it is used.

Are there any requirements specified in case that collecting a file fails?
There are 8 Fallacies of distributed computing, one of them says 'Network is reliable'.
What if this fails? Should data collection be ignored? Except for error message there will be no indication that file did not collected. 

Maybe we should retry the request? I'm not sure about that though. Anyone?
This javadoc gives no value, please remove it.

Also, please consider using either
1) a fake, in memory file system
2) Java.io.File.createTempFile() or equivalent so we do not leave and pollute the repository nor require any particular file system structure to run tests.

Lombok's @Value would generate all of this.
Worth to consider.

Overall, testing http clients through unit test is pointless.
Http clients care about the interaction that goes through the network and are about IO.
You can't test it in isolation through unit tests properly. You could at most test the internal response transformations if there are any.

This is a perfect place for an integration test suite.

This test is equivalent to assert 1 == 1.

You mock things to return X and then you check that it returned X.

I see no value in such tests.
please do not write prints in tests

This test is equivalent to assert 1 == 1.

You mock things to return X and then you check that it returned X.

I see no value in such tests.

If you need to use reflection to test something then you you need to fix your public interface of the class, rather than use reflection.

Please redesign this.

I personally think that there are too many dependencies here, some of them clash, some of them are provided by spring's starters already, some of them I think are unneeded.

Can you please check whether this could be sanitized?
I'd advice to use spring's starters as much as possible.

Why 2 loggings dependencies?
Either use logback or log4j, there are 2 different slf4j providers I believe and there is no reason to include both.

do we use testng specific things here anywhere?
JUnit is already available.

Also, scope test
2.0.4 is out I think. I guess there is nothing that stops us from upgrading at that point.

You could get away with that using something like Wiremock, 
but you would need a Contract tests through consumer driven contract design to verify your stubs are correct but that is too much for current ONAP desgin I guess.
Proper integration/inter-component tests would still be superior.

Surely a method that collects a given file *should* be pure? The only side-effect currently is the writing of said file; is that considered non-pure for this type of use? i.e. given the same parameters it should always write the same file in the same way. With respect to unit testing concerns, production implementation should not be dictated by testing (FYI PowerMock allows mocking of static methods).

I don't get it.
Comment says that we should use any free port, which is reasonable, but then you set it here to a hardcoded value of PORT = 8021.
Do not use PowerMock. Look at Mockito 2.x
Why e.toString()? maybe just log whole exception or e.getMessage() ?
dependency versions should be declared in dependencyManagement section in parent pom

Could you set ftps/sftpClient only in constructor and change them as private final ? It looks like you are setting them twice by @Autowire annotation. I think that the better solution would be to keep @Authowired annotation only above constructor.

I think that the better solution would be return Flux<ConsumerDmaapModel>. One of difference between mono and flux is that Mono returns 0..1 "objects", Flux 0...N
Then you might pass them outside for next processing (I mean outside of FileCollector). For this purpose argument for this function can be Flux<FileData> and then you can process them by using flux api methods
anyway this function must be refactored (dividing them into smaller parts)
if you have got logger I can not understand why are you using java sysout. Everything can be redirected to console or file :)

For this you can change return type object from <Mono<ArrayList<FileData>> [0 or 1 object inside Mono] to Flux<FileData>. This can be the right way for reactive workflow. It means that workflow of this class have to be refactored
Mono<ArrayList<T>> should be changed to Flux<T> (reactive processing)
Do you need this spring annotation?
Don't block, process them as Mono<Type> Flux<Type> using lambda expression
Always will be 200. What if something going wrong?
Yes, it is used in ScheduledXmlContextITest.java

We will be looking in to this when taking care of the error handling properly. At the moment it has not been properly specified in the use case, so this will have to wait until after this patch set has been commited.

We will be looking in to this when taking care of the error handling properly. At the moment it has not been properly specified in the use case, so this will have to wait until after this patch set has been commited.

Should be refactored to use wrappers to make it possible to mock file system and ftp server.

Please move it out of <!--TESTS DEPENDENCIES --> section :)

typo, also variable name should rather be something like consumerDMaaPModels. We usually try not to embed information about the type in variable name since the type is known here anyway
